notes: Gravity for WHEN YOU'RE READY. The Dean scenario — the night Mr. D. Dean arrives at table 14 with the offer that is also a threshold. The brunch service continues around him but the gravity now presses on the corner of the dining room where the suit is sitting. DOUBT pulses higher than in static_bloom. BLOOM still ticks but the room is subtly off. The third kicks more intently when Nicola is near table 14.

deck.title: the bell above the door
deck.flavor: rings the way it rang in 1962. dante's father did not replace it. dante has not replaced it. an out-of-town suit walks in.

deck.title: you seat him at fourteen
deck.flavor: the corner booth two down from paul's usual. river-facing. plain face. plain shoes. the lapel-smoothing gesture of a man who once cared deeply about appearance.

deck.title: the suit does not order
deck.flavor: at 7:48. at 8:14. at 8:42. each time he says: just a moment more. the first time with the polite half-smile. the third time without.

deck.title: hector does not cross himself
deck.flavor: when you pass the line. he has, in six weeks, mostly stopped doing it when you walk by. the tic was about the woman you had been, not the woman you have, now, become.

deck.title: sammy pours himself one
deck.flavor: behind the bar. he knows when to. the boat's small permitted ritual. you note. you do not, this time, comment.

deck.title: the third kicks at fourteen
deck.flavor: you are taking the order at table 11. you pass within four feet of the suit. the kick is harder than her usual. you keep walking. you note.

deck.title: gloria covers the room around him
deck.flavor: she has, instinctively, started taking the tables around fourteen. she does not, today, say why. the not-saying is the saying.

deck.title: the side door is oiled
deck.flavor: you note this passing the bar to take a table's order. the side door has, for years, squeaked. tonight it does not. you have, this week, not seen the maintenance log.

deck.title: dante descends early
deck.flavor: 9:38. six minutes early on his Friday rounds. he walks the floor. he pauses at fourteen. he passes the booth without stopping. he goes to the bar. he drinks the half-bourbon standing.

deck.title: you bring the check presenter
deck.flavor: to fourteen. he has not, all evening, ordered. he nods. you stand at the table for a beat. he is looking at the river through the leaded window.

deck.title: the hundred in the presenter
deck.flavor: a hundred-dollar bill. clean fold. weighing the small folded note. he stands. he smooths his lapels.

deck.title: the polaroid in the kenz house
deck.flavor: philip calls the stand at 9:14 asking if you know what to do with a polaroid that arrived in their mailbox today. the call is, in your private accounting, related to fourteen.

deck.title: alberto arrives off-schedule
deck.flavor: 9:32. he is, this Friday, not due for a week. he comes up the gangway. he does not stop at the stand. he goes straight to the iron stair.

deck.title: he exits through the side door
deck.flavor: near the river. the door that is supposed to be staff-only. he moves with the unhurried economy of a man who knew his way around the boat better than he had any reason to.

deck.title: the folded note remains
deck.flavor: on the table. you walk over. you read it standing. the note says: when you're ready. a phone number below it. you do not, this friday, dial it. you put the note in the apron pocket. you go back to the floor.
